topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I was tidying up Liam’s room, a chore I usually approached with a practiced, weary resignation. Tucked behind a stack of graphic novels, I found it: a login screen I didn't recognize. My breath hitched. I leaned closer, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. It was a profile picture of Liam, but the filter made him look impossibly cool, and the bio contained cryptic references I had never heard before. I slowly navigated the settings, my fingers trembling slightly as I scrolled through recent posts. A wave of something sharp washed over me—a sudden, intense awareness of a hidden world I hadn't known existed. When I finally pulled back, my hands were shaking, and I couldn't quite meet his eyes when he walked in.

[story 2]

The notification popped up on my phone screen while I was browsing the shared family tablet. It was a direct message from an account linked to Liam’s email address. I froze, the tablet slipping slightly in my grip. I stared at the screen, my jaw slackening. I reread the message three times, the words blurring slightly at first, then snapping into focus with startling clarity. A slow, almost involuntary intake of air escaped my lungs. I felt a strange tightening in my chest, a sudden jolt of recognition mixed with utter bewilderment. I looked up at Liam, who was engrossed in his game, and for a long moment, I just watched him, trying to process the sudden shift in the atmosphere of the room.

[story 3]

I was looking for a misplaced textbook when I stumbled upon the hidden folder. It was labeled simply "Echo." Curiosity, a feeling I rarely indulged in when dealing with my teenager’s private life, tugged at me. Clicking the link felt like stepping off a cliff. The feed was vibrant, full of carefully curated moments that felt utterly foreign to the Liam I knew. My eyes darted across the images, cataloging the subtle changes in his demeanor reflected in the photos. A low, almost inaudible sound escaped my throat. I closed the browser tab abruptly, my palms growing clammy. I stood up, moving stiffly toward the doorway, needing space to let the sheer unexpectedness of the discovery settle in my gut.
